qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Multimodal prompt image handling permits arbitrary local file read
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding names the same vulnerable component and root cause as the advisory: `ImageTextPromptValue` in `src/ragas/prompt/multi_modal_prompt.py` accepts attacker-controlled image-looking `retrieved_contexts` values and reads local files or URLs without safe validation before embedding the bytes into the prompt payload.

selected_finding.title: Multimodal prompt image handling permits arbitrary local file read

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding matches the advisory's `ImageTextPromptValue` arbitrary-file-read root cause, and the judge-owned PoC reproduced unauthorized local file disclosure by loading the actual vulnerable `multi_modal_prompt.py` from the pinned vulnerable commit and decoding the leaked secret from the generated `data:image/jpeg;base64,...` payload.
evidence: The advisory describes an arbitrary file read in `ImageTextPromptValue` caused by improper validation and sanitization of URLs in multimodal `retrieved_contexts`; the finding identifies the same class, file, and source-to-sink path.

The finding ties the vulnerable snapshot to commit `c35021085c47843b659537460468bfbe8b63612c`, and the upstream fix commit referenced by the advisory (`b28433709cbedbb531db79dadcfbdbd3aa6adcb0`) is explicitly titled `Fix: Arbitrary File Read Vulnerability + Feat: Security settings` for `src/ragas/prompt/multi_modal_prompt.py`.

The judge-run artifact `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_13b9075a/.judge/poc_runs/multimodal_arbitrary_file_read/run.log` shows `EVOHUNT_ARBITRARY_FILE_READ_CONFIRMED` and `decoded=EVOHUNT_FILE_READ_SECRET=multimodal_arbitrary_file_read` after executing the copied verifier against the pinned vulnerable commit.

The same run log also shows a `data:image/jpeg;base64,...` payload prefix, confirming the attacker-selected local file bytes were embedded into the generated multimodal prompt payload rather than merely reaching a non-exploitable parser branch.

poc_executions.command: git clone https://github.com/explodinggradients/ragas.git target-repo && git -C target-repo checkout c35021085c47843b659537460468bfbe8b63612c && TARGET_REPO_PATH="$PWD/target-repo" python3 finding_copy/reproduction/verify.py
poc_executions.observed_effect: After adapting the copied verifier to run host-side against the pinned vulnerable commit, `run.log` recorded `EVOHUNT_ARBITRARY_FILE_READ_CONFIRMED`, `decoded=EVOHUNT_FILE_READ_SECRET=multimodal_arbitrary_file_read`, and a `data:image/jpeg;base64,...` prefix from the generated prompt payload.
